description_long: This is a small, single-propeller floatplane with a classic high-wing monoplane design, featuring a predominantly white fuselage accented by red wingtips and a red band near the nose. The registration 'CF-AQV' is prominently displayed in black on the wings and fuselage sides. The aircraft is equipped with twin floats for water landings, and its engine is mounted at the nose, with a visible propeller and exposed engine components. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The cockpit is forward-mounted and appears to be enclosed, with windows visible on the side. The overall design suggests a utilitarian aircraft suitable for short takeoff and landing operations on water.
description: A small, high-wing floatplane with a white body, red accents, and twin floats for water landings. It features a single nose-mounted propeller and a forward, enclosed cockpit.
description_view_0: Side view showing the twin floats, exposed nose engine, and registration 'CF-AQV' on the fuselage and wing.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the high-wing configuration, twin floats, and nose-mounted propeller.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with clear visibility of the floats, engine, and registration on the wing and fuselage.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and the full wingspan.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing monoplane with straight wings and no visible winglets; slight dihedral angle present.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with exposed propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Twin fixed floats for water landings, no visible wheels.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with side windows, likely single or dual seat.
